Many decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols, which rely on hybrid structures like upgradeable smart contracts or centralized front ends, could fall into a regulatory gray zone.
Altura’s Pinnock noted that while the platform operates non-custodial strategies, certain features might still attract regulatory scrutiny. Taran Dhillon, head of digital assets at the decentralized impact investment platform Kula, described the exemption as insufficiently clear, leaving developers in “regulatory limbo.” This prolonged uncertainty could drive innovative projects offshore, undermining the EU’s goal of becoming a global crypto hub.
EU regulators maintain that MiCA balances innovation with investor protection. The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) emphasized that the framework was designed to be proportionate to the risks posed by different firms. Smaller players are not expected to meet the same requirements as systemically important entities, according to ESMA. However, the agency supports centralizing supervision for major cross-border exchanges, arguing it would streamline oversight and reduce regulatory arbitrage.
Not all agree. Malta’s Financial Services Authority (MFSA) warned that a one-size-fits-all approach might overlook local market nuances, especially given MiCA’s relatively recent implementation. Source: https://Blockchain.News/news/mica-deadline-small-crypto-firms
item.content:encoded:
EU's MiCA regulation demands compliance by July 1, 2026, forcing smaller crypto firms to adapt or exit as costs and consolidation rise.
The clock is ticking for crypto firms in the European Union as the transitional period for the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation ends on July 1, 2026. Smaller players are under mounting pressure to secure compliance or cease operations within the bloc. MiCA’s sweeping rules, designed to harmonize crypto-asset oversight across the EU, are raising compliance costs and could trigger market consolidation, particularly among lightly capitalized firms.
The regulation, which came into force in June 2023, requires crypto-asset service providers (CASPs) to obtain licenses to continue serving EU clients. While larger, well-established firms like CoinJar have already secured licenses—viewing MiCA as a necessary framework for legitimacy—many smaller operators are struggling to meet the authorization, governance, and reporting requirements.
In Poland, where approximately 2,000 virtual asset service providers (VASPs) operate, only one—Ari10—has successfully obtained a MiCA license, according to its founder, Mateusz Kara. Speaking to Cointelegraph, Kara warned that compliance costs and organizational requirements leave little room for smaller players, potentially forcing many to shutter. This echoes patterns seen in Japan, where post-2018 licensing reforms drove smaller exchanges out of the market.
“The market is heading for consolidation,” said Matthew Pinnock, COO of the decentralized finance platform Altura. He added that larger, well-capitalized exchanges and custodians are better positioned to weather MiCA’s regulatory demands, leaving smaller or hybrid projects at a disadvantage.
Despite MiCA’s exemption for fully decentralized services under Recital 22, industry insiders argue the definition remains too vague. Many decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols, which rely on hybrid structures like upgradeable smart contracts or centralized front ends, could fall into a regulatory gray zone.
Altura’s Pinnock noted that while the platform operates non-custodial strategies, certain features might still attract regulatory scrutiny. Taran Dhillon, head of digital assets at the decentralized impact investment platform Kula, described the exemption as insufficiently clear, leaving developers in “regulatory limbo.” This prolonged uncertainty could drive innovative projects offshore, undermining the EU’s goal of becoming a global crypto hub.
EU regulators maintain that MiCA balances innovation with investor protection. The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) emphasized that the framework was designed to be proportionate to the risks posed by different firms. Smaller players are not expected to meet the same requirements as systemically important entities, according to ESMA. However, the agency supports centralizing supervision for major cross-border exchanges, arguing it would streamline oversight and reduce regulatory arbitrage.
Not all agree. Malta’s Financial Services Authority (MFSA) warned that a one-size-fits-all approach might overlook local market nuances, especially given MiCA’s relatively recent implementation.
While MiCA represents a compliance hurdle for smaller firms, early adopters like CoinJar’s CEO, Asher Tan, see it as a filter that will ultimately strengthen the market. “MiCA brings crypto in line with serious financial frameworks,” Tan said, adding that the regulation encourages long-term value creation over speculative activity.
For firms that can adapt, MiCA offers a clear, passportable path to scale across the EU. However, as the July 1 deadline approaches, the industry faces a critical test: whether MiCA will cement Europe as a trusted crypto hub or push the next wave of innovation elsewhere remains to be seen.

item.title: APT Primed for Bull Trap Rally to $1.20 Before $0.75 Crash | Aptos shows classic distribution patterns at $0.97 with derivatives positioning screaming... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260422-prediction-apt-primed-for-bull-trap-rally-to
item.description:
Original title: APT Primed for Bull Trap Rally to $1.20 Before $0.75 Crash Summary: Aptos shows classic distribution patterns at $0.97 with derivatives positioning screaming institutional exit. Expect dead cat bounce to $1.20 resistance followed by violent 35% correction to $0.75 Article text: Aptos shows classic distribution patterns at $0.97 with derivatives positioning screaming institutional exit. Expect dead cat bounce to $1.20 resistance followed by violent 35% correction to $0.75 ...
Aptos painted the perfect bull trap setup today, climbing 3.42% to $0.97 while smart money quietly heads for the exits. The taker buy/sell ratio at 0.72 exposes the underlying weakness - aggressive selling dominates despite the green candle. This divergence between surface price action and actual order flow marks textbook distribution, not the accumulation retail traders are betting on.
The derivatives market tells an even uglier story. Top traders maintain 62.8% long positions while retail sits at 58.6% long, creating a dangerous consensus trade. When positioning becomes this crowded with declining open interest down 0.76%, the stage is set for maximum pain. The neutral funding rate at 0.01% removes any immediate squeeze pressure, giving institutions perfect conditions to time their exit.
APT's current positioning near the upper Bollinger Band at 86% creates the ideal launching pad for false hope. The MACD histogram sits at absolute zero while RSI hovers at 56 - not oversold enough to prevent further upside but perfectly positioned for momentum failure. This combination typically produces sharp rallies that die spectacular deaths.
The moving average structure reveals the bigger picture damage. While APT managed to reclaim the 7-day SMA at $0.96, it trades 47% below the 200-day SMA at $1.83. Short-term averages clustering around $0.90-$0.93 will act as gravitational pull once this bounce exhausts itself. The massive gap between current price and longer-term trend represents pure air pocket territory.
Market structure points toward a calculated two-phase move designed to trap both sides. Phase one targets $1.20 resistance where I expect violent rejection within one week. This level represents the final liquidity grab before institutional selling overwhelms diminishing buyer demand.
The optimal short entry zone sits at $1.15-$1.20 with tight stops at $1.25. Initial resistance appears at $1.01, but the real money comes from positioning early for the second phase crash toward $0.75 strong support. This downside target represents a 35-40% correction that will eliminate every support level between current price and major demand.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
Invalidation occurs only if APT reclaims and holds above $1.25 for more than 48 hours, suggesting genuine institutional accumulation rather than distribution. Given current derivative positioning and the absence of fundamental catalysts, this scenario remains highly unlikely.
The most probable path sees APT touching $1.20 within two weeks, followed by the kind of correction that reminds everyone why position sizing matters. The setup carries high conviction, but markets can remain irrational far longer than most traders can maintain their margin calls. Trade accordingly.

item.title: Russia Advances Crypto Bill as First Reading Passes State Duma | Russia's parliament moves forward with a crypto bill mandating licensed intermediaries an... https://Blockchain.News/news/russia-crypto-bill-first-reading
item.description:
Original title: Russia Advances Crypto Bill as First Reading Passes State Duma Summary: Russia's parliament moves forward with a crypto bill mandating licensed intermediaries and imposing strict penalties on unregistered platforms. Article text: Russia's parliament moves forward with a crypto bill mandating licensed intermediaries and imposing strict penalties on unregistered platforms.
Russia’s State Duma has approved the first reading of a sweeping crypto regulation bill, setting the stage for significant changes to the country’s digital asset market. The draft legislation, titled “On Digital Currency and Digital Rights” (Bill No. 1194918-8), proposes channeling all cryptocurrency transactions through licensed intermediaries under the supervision of the Bank of Russia. If passed into law, these rules could take effect as early as July 2026, with unlicensed platforms banned outright starting July 2027.
The bill, along with three related proposals, aims to establish a regulated crypto ecosystem while introducing investment caps for retail traders, mandatory regulatory compliance, and strict penalties for violations. Retail investors, for instance, would face a purchase limit of 300,000 rubles (approximately $4,000) annually through any single licensed intermediary. Only “most liquid digital currencies”—those with a market cap exceeding 5 trillion rubles ($66.6 billion) and a five-year trading history—would qualify for trading under the proposed rules.
A related proposal, Bill No. 1209607-8, seeks to impose criminal penalties on operators of unregistered crypto services, including imprisonment and fines. However, the Russian Supreme Court has expressed reservations about this measure, calling it premature given the absence of a fully adopted regulatory framework. Critics argue that pushing for criminal enforcement without clear guidelines could lead to confusion and unintended consequences for the burgeoning sector.
Russia’s approach to cryptocurrency regulation has shifted markedly in recent years. While crypto ownership and trading remain legal, domestic payments using digital assets are prohibited. The Bank of Russia has taken a central role in shaping the country’s digital currency policies, including its rollout of the digital ruble. Recent legislative efforts build on a 2023 framework that formally recognized cryptocurrencies as property, but the new bills aim to tighten oversight further.
These developments are part of a broader strategy to reduce the crypto market’s “grey zone,” align with anti-financial crime initiatives, and mitigate risks to consumers. However, industry participants warn that over-regulation could have the opposite effect, driving crypto activity underground rather than encouraging compliance. The emphasis on licensed intermediaries also raises questions about the future of peer-to-peer trading in Russia.
For international observers, Russia’s crypto policies are particularly significant given the geopolitical backdrop of sanctions. While domestic crypto use remains tightly controlled, digital assets are reportedly being explored as a tool for cross-border transactions and international trade.
The bill must pass additional readings before becoming law, meaning its final provisions could still change. Meanwhile, the Bank of Russia is likely to play a pivotal role in defining which assets qualify as “most liquid” and setting compliance standards.
As global crypto markets watch Russia’s regulatory moves closely, traders should keep an eye on potential enforcement timelines and any shifts in market accessibility. If implemented, these laws could reshape the dynamics of crypto trading across one of the largest markets in Eastern Europe.

item.title: LINK Eyes $11-12 Breakout as Smart Money Loads Up During Price Compression | Chainlink trades near $9.52 while 69% of top traders position long, setting up a potentia... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260422-price-prediction-link-eyes-11-12-breakout-as-smart
item.description:
Original title: LINK Eyes $11-12 Breakout as Smart Money Loads Up During Price Compression Summary: Chainlink trades near $9.52 while 69% of top traders position long, setting up a potential run to $11-12 as the current consolidation pattern approaches its breaking point. Article text: Chainlink trades near $9.52 while 69% of top traders position long, setting up a potential run to $11-12 as the current consolidation pattern approaches its breaking point.
TITLE: LINK Eyes $11-12 Breakout as Smart Money Loads Up During Price Compression
The price action around $9.52 tells a story of deliberate accumulation rather than indecision. While LINK grinds sideways between key levels, the underlying mechanics suggest this consolidation is building pressure for the next major move higher.
The RSI holding steady at 56.56 reflects a market in equilibrium - neither sellers dominating with oversold readings nor buyers getting carried away into overbought territory. This neutral momentum reading during sideways price action often precedes directional breakouts when combined with other confirming factors.
More telling is how price continues to test the $9.64-$9.76 resistance zone without getting firmly rejected. Each test that fails to produce significant selling pressure weakens that ceiling and increases the likelihood of an eventual breakthrough.
The derivatives market reveals where sophisticated traders are placing their bets. Top traders maintain a 2.23:1 long-to-short ratio with 69% positioned bullish - a stark contrast to typical retail behavior during sideways markets. These aren't momentum chasers; they're positioned ahead of the anticipated move.
Daily spot volume on Binance alone hits $24.2 million, indicating sustained institutional interest despite the lack of dramatic price movement. The $82 million in open interest holding steady through recent minor declines shows leveraged positions aren't getting forced out by normal volatility.
The balanced taker buy/sell ratio at 0.945 confirms neither panic selling nor euphoric buying is driving current action. Instead, it points to methodical accumulation by patient capital willing to build positions during this quiet phase.
The compression between $8.40 and $9.60 has lasted long enough to create meaningful technical significance. When volatility contracts this much - reflected in the daily ATR of $0.42 - markets typically respond with sharp directional moves once a catalyst emerges.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
A decisive break above $9.76 would likely trigger algorithmic buying programs and force covering from the minority short positions. This mechanical response could accelerate price movement toward the $10 psychological level, which then becomes the gateway to higher targets.
The MACD sitting near zero while price holds in the upper portion of its recent range suggests momentum is coiled rather than dissipating. This combination often precedes explosive moves when the technical setup finally resolves.
The path forward depends on clearing that $9.76 resistance with conviction. A breakout above $10 opens the door to the $11-12 zone within the next month, supported by the current smart money positioning and compressed volatility environment.
Should the bullish scenario unfold, the initial target sits around $11.50 based on the width of the current consolidation pattern. A secondary target near $12 becomes viable if momentum sustains and retail interest follows institutional positioning.
The alternative scenario requires a breakdown below $9.12 support, which would invalidate the accumulation thesis and likely target the $8.40 area. However, current positioning data and volume characteristics suggest this remains the less probable outcome given how smart money is positioned.
Risk/reward clearly favors the upside here. The combination of patient institutional accumulation, compressed volatility, and a well-defined technical setup creates conditions that historically resolve in favor of the direction where sophisticated money is positioned.
Chainlink trades near $9.52 while 69% of top traders position long, setting up a potential run to $11-12 as the current consolidation pattern approaches its breaking point.

The price action around $9.52 tells a story of deliberate accumulation rather than indecision. While LINK grinds sideways between key levels, the underlying mechanics suggest this consolidation is building pressure for the next major move higher.
The RSI holding steady at 56.56 reflects a market in equilibrium - neither sellers dominating with oversold readings nor buyers getting carried away into overbought territory. This neutral momentum reading during sideways price action often precedes directional breakouts when combined with other confirming factors. TITLE: LINK Eyes $11-12 Breakout as Smart Money Loads Up During Price Compression
The price action around $9.52 tells a story of deliberate accumulation rather than indecision. While LINK grinds sideways between key levels, the underlying mechanics suggest this consolidation is building pressure for the next major move higher.
The RSI holding steady at 56.56 reflects a market in equilibrium - neither sellers dominating with oversold readings nor buyers getting carried away into overbought territory. This neutral momentum reading during sideways price action often precedes directional breakouts when combined with other confirming factors.
More telling is how price continues to test the $9.64-$9.76 resistance zone without getting firmly rejected. Each test that fails to produce significant selling pressure weakens that ceiling and increases the likelihood of an eventual breakthrough.
The derivatives market reveals where sophisticated traders are placing their bets. Top traders maintain a 2.23:1 long-to-short ratio with 69% positioned bullish - a stark contrast to typical retail behavior during sideways markets. These aren't momentum chasers; they're positioned ahead of the anticipated move.

item.title: AVAX Tests $11 Breakout as Whale Activity Surges Above Key Support | Avalanche consolidates near $9.50 with technical indicators suggesting a push toward $11... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260422-prediction-avax-tests-11-breakout-as-whale-activity
item.description:
Original title: AVAX Tests $11 Breakout as Whale Activity Surges Above Key Support Summary: Avalanche consolidates near $9.50 with technical indicators suggesting a push toward $11 within two weeks. Strong support at $9.23 and neutral momentum readings create favorable conditions for upsi Article text: Avalanche consolidates near $9.50 with technical indicators suggesting a push toward $11 within two weeks. Strong support at $9.23 and neutral momentum readings create favorable conditions for upsi...
Avalanche trades at $9.51 within a tight consolidation range, bounded by resistance at $9.74 and support at $9.23. The RSI reading of 53.17 sits comfortably in neutral territory, providing room for upward movement without immediately triggering overbought conditions. Meanwhile, the flatlining MACD histogram indicates momentum remains compressed, typical of assets preparing for directional moves.
The Bollinger Band positioning shows AVAX at 0.71 of the range between bands, suggesting underlying strength despite recent sideways action. Price continues holding above the 20-day SMA at $9.29, which has served as a reliable support level throughout recent trading sessions.
Daily trading volume of $28.1 million reflects steady institutional interest without excessive speculative activity. The derivatives positioning shows institutional traders maintaining a bullish bias, though funding rates remain near neutral at 0.01%, indicating balanced leverage across long and short positions.
Open interest has increased by 0.16%, suggesting new capital entering positions rather than existing traders simply rotating. This fresh positioning often precedes significant price movements as market participants establish stakes ahead of anticipated volatility.
The convergence of neutral RSI positioning and compressed MACD creates conditions favorable for upward momentum. A break above $9.74 resistance would likely target the $10.50 area initially, with extension potential toward $11.50 if buying pressure sustains.
The technical setup resembles classic accumulation patterns where controlled volatility gives way to trending moves. AVAX's ability to maintain current levels despite broader market uncertainty demonstrates relative strength that could translate into outperformance.
Below current support at $9.23, the next significant level sits at $8.94. A breakdown through these areas would signal weakness and potentially trigger further selling pressure from leveraged positions.
The key resistance at $9.74 has held multiple tests recently, making a decisive break above this level crucial for confirming bullish momentum. Without this breakout, AVAX may remain range-bound between current support and resistance levels.
Technical indicators align for potential upside movement toward $11 over the next 14 days, assuming current support levels hold. The $10.50 area represents the initial target following any breakout above $9.74 resistance.
Traders should monitor the $9.23 support level closely, as sustained trading below this area would invalidate near-term bullish expectations and suggest further downside toward $8.94.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.

item.title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Push Boundaries of AI with Rubin GPUs | NVIDIA's Vera Rubin GPUs and Google Cloud's AI infrastructure pave the way for agentic an... https://Blockchain.News/news/nvidia-google-cloud-rubin-ai-factories
item.description:
Original title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Push Boundaries of AI with Rubin GPUs Summary: NVIDIA's Vera Rubin GPUs and Google Cloud's AI infrastructure pave the way for agentic and physical AI at unprecedented scales. Article text: NVIDIA's Vera Rubin GPUs and Google Cloud's AI infrastructure pave the way for agentic and physical AI at unprecedented scales.
NVIDIA (NASDAQ: NVDA) and Google Cloud have unveiled a groundbreaking expansion of their decade-long partnership, targeting the next generation of AI applications. Announced at Google Cloud Next 2026 in Las Vegas, the collaboration revolves around NVIDIA’s Vera Rubin-powered A5X instances and Google Cloud’s AI infrastructure, enabling the creation of "AI factories" capable of scaling to nearly 1 million Rubin GPUs.
These AI factories aim to advance agentic and physical AI—systems designed to reason, plan, and act autonomously—while cutting costs and energy use. NVIDIA’s Rubin GPU platform, part of the cutting-edge Blackwell architecture, offers up to four times the inference throughput of its predecessor, the H100, and consumes 25 times less energy for trillion-parameter models. This makes it a crucial leap forward in reducing the cost and environmental impact of AI workloads.
The A5X instances, powered by NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale systems, promise a 10x reduction in inference cost per token and a 10x increase in token throughput per megawatt compared to earlier models. These instances can scale up to 80,000 Rubin GPUs in a single-site cluster and 960,000 GPUs across multisite clusters, offering unparalleled performance for enterprise-scale AI tasks such as training large language models and running real-time inference workloads.
"The next decade of AI will be shaped by the ability to run demanding workloads on integrated, AI-optimized infrastructure," said Mark Lohmeyer, VP and GM of AI and computing infrastructure at Google Cloud. By combining Google Cloud's scalable infrastructure with NVIDIA's Blackwell-based systems, the platform gives enterprises a flexible and cost-effective solution for complex AI workflows.
Beyond infrastructure, the partnership introduces enhancements to NVIDIA’s Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform. Developers can leverage NVIDIA Nemotron and NeMo frameworks to build agentic AI systems capable of managing workflows, simulating environments, and powering robotics. For example, NVIDIA's open models and APIs are now optimized to run in Google Cloud environments, enabling faster training and deployment of agentic AI.
Applications range from cybersecurity tools by CrowdStrike, which fine-tune models for domain-specific threat detection, to industrial solutions like NVIDIA Omniverse and Isaac Sim, which are used to create digital twins and robotics simulations. These tools streamline the transition from design to real-world deployment, spanning industries such as automotive, aerospace, and heavy machinery.
NVIDIA’s Blackwell GPUs bring significant advancements in memory bandwidth, energy efficiency, and tensor performance. These features are crucial for training enormous models and running real-time AI inference. With cloud rental rates for Blackwell GPUs ranging from $2.65/hr to $14.24/hr and complete DGX B200 systems priced at over $500,000, NVIDIA is solidifying its position as the AI hardware leader.
This dominance is reflected in NVIDIA’s current market capitalization of $4.858 trillion, making it the world’s most valuable company. The Vera Rubin platform, with its combination of Rubin GPUs and Vera CPUs, is expected to generate substantial growth opportunities as cloud providers like AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft deploy Rubin-based instances throughout 2026.
This latest collaboration underscores NVIDIA's and Google Cloud's commitment to accelerating the deployment of AI at massive scales. With agentic and physical AI poised to transform industries, enterprises now have access to the tools and infrastructure needed to move from prototyping to production seamlessly. As more developers join the ecosystem—over 90,000 in the past year alone—the potential for innovation is enormous.
For traders and investors, the AI hardware race is a key area to watch. NVIDIA’s ability to maintain its technological lead with products like the Rubin and Blackwell platforms strengthens its growth outlook, while its partnership with Google Cloud adds another layer of defensibility against competitors.
The companies' joint efforts will be showcased in upcoming workshops and demos at Google Cloud Next. With enterprise adoption accelerating, NVIDIA continues to set the standard for the AI era.

item.title: Hong Kong Auctions RMB Sovereign Bonds, Results Due April 22 | Hong Kong Monetary Authority announces tender results for 2-year RMB sovereign bonds, hig... https://Blockchain.News/news/hong-kong-rmb-sovereign-bonds-april-2026
item.description:
Original title: Hong Kong Auctions RMB Sovereign Bonds, Results Due April 22 Summary: Hong Kong Monetary Authority announces tender results for 2-year RMB sovereign bonds, highlighting RMB's growing role in global finance. Article text: Hong Kong Monetary Authority announces tender results for 2-year RMB sovereign bonds, highlighting RMB's growing role in global finance.
The Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) is set to announce the results of its latest tender for 2-year RMB-denominated sovereign bonds on April 22, 2026. This issuance, part of China's ongoing push to internationalize the renminbi, underscores Hong Kong's unique role as a bridge between mainland China and global financial markets.
The bonds, issued by the Ministry of Finance, are a regular feature of Hong Kong’s financial calendar, offering investors exposure to RMB assets in one of the world’s most liquid offshore RMB markets. These tenders are closely watched, not just for their yield levels, but also as a barometer of investor appetite for RMB-denominated debt amid fluctuating global interest rates and geopolitical tensions.
While specific auction details remain under wraps until the official release, recent trends in RMB bond issuance provide some context. According to market analysts, demand for RMB bonds has grown steadily in recent years, supported by China’s stable economic growth and the currency’s inclusion in the IMF’s Special Drawing Rights (SDR) basket since 2016.
Hong Kong has played a pivotal role in this growth. As of late 2023, it accounted for nearly 70% of the global offshore RMB bond market, cementing its status as a key hub for RMB internationalization. The city's robust legal framework, transparent financial markets, and proximity to mainland China make it an attractive venue for these issuances.
For traders and institutional investors, the April 22 results could offer insight into the current yield environment for RMB assets and gauge the impact of broader economic factors, such as U.S. Federal Reserve rate policies and China’s domestic monetary stance, on RMB debt pricing.
Looking ahead, the auction’s outcome might influence future RMB issuance strategies and provide clues about global confidence in China's economic trajectory. Traders will likely assess the bid-to-cover ratio, yield levels, and any deviations from prior auctions to fine-tune their exposure to Chinese sovereign bonds.

According to market analysts, demand for RMB bonds has grown steadily in recent years, supported by China’s stable economic growth and the currency’s inclusion in the IMF’s Special Drawing Rights (SDR) basket since 2016.
Hong Kong has played a pivotal role in this growth. As of late 2023, it accounted for nearly 70% of the global offshore RMB bond market, cementing its status as a key hub for RMB internationalization. The city's robust legal framework, transparent financial markets, and proximity to mainland China make it an attractive venue for these issuances.
For traders and institutional investors, the April 22 results could offer insight into the current yield environment for RMB assets and gauge the impact of broader economic factors, such as U.S. Federal Reserve rate policies and China’s domestic monetary stance, on RMB debt pricing.
Looking ahead, the auction’s outcome might influence future RMB issuance strategies and provide clues about global confidence in China's economic trajectory. item.content:encoded:
Hong Kong Monetary Authority announces tender results for 2-year RMB sovereign bonds, highlighting RMB's growing role in global finance.
The Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) is set to announce the results of its latest tender for 2-year RMB-denominated sovereign bonds on April 22, 2026. This issuance, part of China's ongoing push to internationalize the renminbi, underscores Hong Kong's unique role as a bridge between mainland China and global financial markets.
The bonds, issued by the Ministry of Finance, are a regular feature of Hong Kong’s financial calendar, offering investors exposure to RMB assets in one of the world’s most liquid offshore RMB markets. These tenders are closely watched, not just for their yield levels, but also as a barometer of investor appetite for RMB-denominated debt amid fluctuating global interest rates and geopolitical tensions.
While specific auction details remain under wraps until the official release, recent trends in RMB bond issuance provide some context. According to market analysts, demand for RMB bonds has grown steadily in recent years, supported by China’s stable economic growth and the currency’s inclusion in the IMF’s Special Drawing Rights (SDR) basket since 2016.
Hong Kong has played a pivotal role in this growth. As of late 2023, it accounted for nearly 70% of the global offshore RMB bond market, cementing its status as a key hub for RMB internationalization. The city's robust legal framework, transparent financial markets, and proximity to mainland China make it an attractive venue for these issuances.
For traders and institutional investors, the April 22 results could offer insight into the current yield environment for RMB assets and gauge the impact of broader economic factors, such as U.S. Federal Reserve rate policies and China’s domestic monetary stance, on RMB debt pricing.
Looking ahead, the auction’s outcome might influence future RMB issuance strategies and provide clues about global confidence in China's economic trajectory. Traders will likely assess the bid-to-cover ratio, yield levels, and any deviations from prior auctions to fine-tune their exposure to Chinese sovereign bonds.

item.title: US Admiral Calls Bitcoin Key to Cybersecurity and Power Projection | Admiral Samuel Paparo sees Bitcoin as a strategic tool for U.S. cybersecurity and nationa... https://Blockchain.News/news/us-admiral-bitcoin-cybersecurity-power-projection
item.description:
Original title: US Admiral Calls Bitcoin Key to Cybersecurity and Power Projection Summary: Admiral Samuel Paparo sees Bitcoin as a strategic tool for U.S. cybersecurity and national power, emphasizing its proof-of-work advantages. Article text: Admiral Samuel Paparo sees Bitcoin as a strategic tool for U.S. cybersecurity and national power, emphasizing its proof-of-work advantages.
A top U.S. military official has highlighted Bitcoin (BTC) as a critical tool for national security, with applications extending far beyond its monetary use. Speaking at a Senate Armed Services Committee hearing on April 21, Admiral Samuel Paparo, the commander of the U.S. Indo-Pacific Command, described Bitcoin’s proof-of-work technology as pivotal to strengthening cybersecurity and projecting American power globally.
“It is a valuable computer science tool, as a power projection,” said Paparo. He explained that Bitcoin’s decentralized and energy-intensive proof-of-work system imposes significant costs on potential attackers, making it a strong defensive asset in the escalating arena of cyberwarfare. Paparo added, "Outside of the economic formulation of it, it has got really important computer science applications for cybersecurity."
Paparo’s remarks come as the U.S. faces mounting threats from state-linked cyber adversaries like North Korea’s Lazarus Group, which has stolen billions in crypto to fund its weapons programs. With Bitcoin trading at $75,508 as of April 22 and a market cap of $1.51 trillion, the digital asset continues to grow as a strategic resource. Analysts have long argued that Bitcoin’s decentralized infrastructure could play a key role in securing critical data and communications, particularly in military and government operations.
The discussion at the Senate hearing centered on the Indo-Pacific region’s strategic challenges, including China’s military expansion, conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East, and cyber threats from North Korea. Senator Tommy Tuberville, who questioned Paparo, noted that China’s monetary think tank has also begun treating Bitcoin as a strategic asset. While Paparo didn’t directly address U.S.-China Bitcoin competition, he emphasized that Bitcoin’s “peer-to-peer zero-trust transfer of value” aligns with broader U.S. national power goals.
Admiral Paparo’s comments echo earlier statements by U.S. Space Force member Jason Lowery, who in December 2023 argued that proof-of-work blockchains like Bitcoin could safeguard not just financial systems but also critical communications. Lowery warned that underestimating Bitcoin’s cybersecurity applications could undermine national security.
Amid these strategic considerations, U.S. lawmakers are pushing to bolster domestic Bitcoin mining capabilities. In March 2026, Senators Bill Cassidy and Cynthia Lummis introduced the Mined in America Act, aiming to reduce reliance on foreign-manufactured mining equipment by incentivizing U.S.-based production. The legislation seeks to codify the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, a concept first introduced under the Trump administration.
Currently, the U.S. holds the largest Bitcoin reserves among nation-states and dominates the global Bitcoin hashrate. However, vulnerabilities in the supply chain for mining hardware remain a concern. By addressing these gaps, policymakers hope to secure Bitcoin’s role in national defense and economic stability.
Admiral Paparo’s advocacy for Bitcoin underscores its growing recognition as more than an investment asset. Its role as a defensive tool in cyberspace could reshape how the U.S. approaches both military strategy and technological innovation. With adversaries increasingly leveraging cyberattacks to destabilize infrastructure, Bitcoin’s cryptographic architecture offers a unique layer of protection for sensitive systems.
For traders, these developments signal Bitcoin’s evolving narrative. What was once dismissed as a speculative asset now carries explicit endorsements from U.S. military leaders for its strategic utility. This shift could influence long-term institutional adoption and reinforce Bitcoin’s position as a geopolitical asset.

item.title: AI-Powered Geotechnical Data Platform Transforms NZ Infrastructure | Beca’s BEYON platform, integrated with Microsoft Azure, is revolutionizing New Zealand's... https://Blockchain.News/news/ai-geotechnical-data-new-zealand
item.description:
Original title: AI-Powered Geotechnical Data Platform Transforms NZ Infrastructure Summary: Beca’s BEYON platform, integrated with Microsoft Azure, is revolutionizing New Zealand's geotechnical data management, enhancing safety and efficiency. Article text: Beca’s BEYON platform, integrated with Microsoft Azure, is revolutionizing New Zealand's geotechnical data management, enhancing safety and efficiency.
New Zealand’s efforts to rebuild safer and smarter after the devastating 2011 Christchurch earthquake have received a major boost with the integration of artificial intelligence and digital twin technology into geotechnical data management. The National Geotechnical Database (NZGD), now hosted on Beca’s BEYON platform and powered by Microsoft Azure, is enabling faster, more informed decision-making for engineers and infrastructure planners.
Originally created in 2013 to centralize fragmented geotechnical data, the NZGD was recently overhauled with modern cloud and AI capabilities. In November 2024, Beca relaunched the platform with an SQL database on Microsoft Azure, emphasizing scalability, security, and advanced spatial analytics. This move has transformed the database into a critical tool for managing New Zealand’s infrastructure projects.
The Christchurch earthquake underscored the dire need for accessible subsurface data. Engineers struggled to make decisions on whether to repair, demolish, or rebuild structures due to the fragmented nature of geotechnical information. "Collating data was a massive challenge," said Beca CEO Amelia Linzey. "Creating a common repository as a national asset became a necessity."
Today, the NZGD hosts over 168,000 geotechnical tests, and its AI-powered features allow users to query and extract critical data using natural language—saving time and reducing errors. Stephen Witherden, Beca’s Product Strategy Manager, noted that the AI assistant, developed at Microsoft Foundry, accelerates data access while adhering to strict response guardrails.
Beca’s BEYON platform stands at the heart of this transformation. A digital twin system, BEYON creates virtual replicas of physical assets, consolidating complex data streams into a unified dashboard. This approach allows engineers to simulate scenarios, predict outcomes, and optimize resource use—key for tackling New Zealand's unique geological challenges.
Shifting NZGD to BEYON on Azure has also unlocked new possibilities for collaboration. "Accessibility and scalability are paramount," said Witherden, emphasizing the role of Azure's security features like Entra ID in safeguarding sensitive data. The platform now supports over 4,300 users, from engineers to local authorities, improving infrastructure planning and risk mitigation at a national scale.
Looking ahead, this AI-powered geotechnical system could serve as a model for other countries grappling with infrastructure resilience. By integrating digital twins and AI, Beca is not just managing data but enabling a smarter, more sustainable approach to development.
Beca, founded in 1920 and with over 4,000 employees globally, has positioned BEYON as a versatile tool for industries beyond geotechnical data. From supporting climate adaptation planning to reimagining manufacturing processes, the platform embodies Beca’s vision of sustainable, technology-driven solutions.
For New Zealand, the upgraded NZGD is more than just a database; it’s a critical step toward safer, more efficient infrastructure development, leveraging cutting-edge tools to turn past lessons into future resilience.

item.title: Alchemy Brings $4T Infrastructure to Injective (INJ)Blockchain | Alchemy integrates with Injective, enabling developers to build high-speed, onchain finan... https://Blockchain.News/news/alchemy-launches-on-injective
item.description:
Original title: Alchemy Brings $4T Infrastructure to Injective (INJ)Blockchain Summary: Alchemy integrates with Injective, enabling developers to build high-speed, onchain finance apps with $4 trillion-backed infrastructure. Article text: Alchemy integrates with Injective, enabling developers to build high-speed, onchain finance apps with $4 trillion-backed infrastructure.
Alchemy, the leading blockchain developer platform responsible for over $4 trillion in onchain transactions, has officially launched its RPC infrastructure on Injective, the high-speed, finance-focused layer-1 blockchain. Developers using Alchemy's platform can now seamlessly build on Injective (INJ)without altering their existing workflows, marking a significant expansion for both ecosystems.
For Injective, this integration is a major step in scaling its developer ecosystem. By tapping into Alchemy's established infrastructure, which boasts 99.99% uptime and powers hundreds of thousands of developers, Injective positions itself as a go-to chain for onchain finance applications ranging from trading platforms to real-world asset (RWA) markets. Injective already processes transactions with sub-second finality and charges zero gas fees on trades, providing centralized exchange-level speed with the transparency of blockchain.
Injective's native features, such as its onchain order book and MultiVM architecture, give developers a significant advantage. Builders can write in either Solidity or Wasm while interacting natively across virtual machines via the MultiVM Token Standard. This enables seamless migrations for Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Cosmos-based developers without requiring a new toolchain.
Additionally, by leveraging Injective’s infrastructure, developers gain access to shared liquidity, advanced order types, and MEV-resistant execution from day one. These features are particularly attractive for teams building decentralized trading platforms and lending protocols. The integration with Alchemy ensures these applications run on reliable endpoints, eliminating the need for developers to manage their own nodes or worry about infrastructure scaling during traffic spikes.
Injective's ecosystem is already bustling with activity. Helix, a decentralized exchange, operates spot and derivatives markets that include tokenized gold, crude oil, and pre-IPO perpetuals. Neptune Finance leads in peer-to-peer lending, while Hydro Protocol combines liquid staking with RWA yield strategies. These applications capitalize on Injective's speed and cost-efficiency, and Alchemy's infrastructure will enhance their reliability further.
Additionally, Injective is set to integrate native USDC via Circle's Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol, bringing regulated stablecoins and interoperability with over 30 blockchains. This will strengthen the ecosystem's appeal to institutional players while simplifying cross-chain transactions for developers and users alike.
Alchemy has solidified its place as the "AWS of blockchain," powering major Web3 projects and enterprise solutions. Its platform simplifies blockchain development with tools like Alchemy Build for debugging and Alchemy Monitor for tracking events. Recent expansions include supporting Solana with a $20 million fund and infrastructure partnerships with projects like Katana DeFi Chain and OVHcloud.
This launch on Injective aligns with Alchemy's strategy of supporting high-performance blockchains geared toward financial applications. The integration also underscores a growing trend: developers are increasingly demanding infrastructure that supports not only reliability but also compatibility across ecosystems without workflow disruptions.
Developers can start building on Injective immediately using Alchemy’s API and Injective’s developer documentation. Testnet faucet support is available for early-stage projects, offering teams a chance to test and iterate before deploying on mainnet.
With Injective’s $76.5 billion in cumulative trading volume and Alchemy's extensive developer base, this partnership has the potential to drive significant innovation in onchain finance. Whether you're building a next-gen trading platform, a lending protocol, or an AI-driven financial application, the tools are now in place to make development faster and more scalable than ever.

item.title: UK Unveils New Payment Rules for Stablecoins, Tokenized Assets | UK Treasury introduces reforms to integrate stablecoins, tokenized deposits into payments... https://Blockchain.News/news/uk-payment-rules-stablecoins-tokenized-assets
item.description:
Original title: UK Unveils New Payment Rules for Stablecoins, Tokenized Assets Summary: UK Treasury introduces reforms to integrate stablecoins, tokenized deposits into payments system, appoints Chris Woolard to lead digital strategy. Article text: UK Treasury introduces reforms to integrate stablecoins, tokenized deposits into payments system, appoints Chris Woolard to lead digital strategy.
The UK government has announced sweeping reforms to its payment regulations, aiming to bring stablecoins, tokenized deposits, and traditional payment systems under a single framework. The move is part of a broader strategy to position the UK as a leader in digital markets and was unveiled during UK Fintech Week on April 21, 2026.
HM Treasury stated that the regulatory overhaul will include legislation to reduce administrative burdens for companies offering stablecoin payment services. This effort seeks to "cement the UK as a world-leading destination for digital assets," according to the announcement. Additionally, the Treasury plans to address payment regulations for artificial intelligence (AI) agents that execute transactions autonomously on behalf of users.
The reforms aim to establish a "single, coherent framework" for payments, encompassing traditional methods alongside stablecoins and tokenized deposits. This builds on prior efforts by the UK to regulate stablecoins, including an April 3, 2026, Treasury report that outlined a framework for incorporating stablecoins into the financial system. The Bank of England and Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) are expected to publish joint guidelines later this year to clarify their approach.
Key features of the initiative include aligning stablecoins with existing financial services regulations, such as the Payment Services Regulations 2017, and ensuring robust oversight of issuance and custody activities under the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000. By integrating tokenized digital assets into traditional financial systems, the UK aims to foster innovation while mitigating risks to consumer protection and financial stability.
To spearhead its digital markets strategy, the UK has appointed Chris Woolard, a former Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) executive, as its digital markets champion. Woolard will support efforts to drive adoption of tokenized assets and strengthen collaboration between the public and private sectors. In his remarks, he emphasized the importance of digitization in maintaining the UK's competitiveness in global financial markets.
The Treasury is also exploring how to regulate payments made by AI agents, reflecting the growing role of automation in financial transactions. Industry stakeholders, such as Philip Belamant, co-founder of FCA-approved fintech Zilch, have highlighted how AI could transform money management by automating payments in the background. Belamant noted that "regulation must evolve to support innovation while maintaining strong consumer protections."
The UK’s proactive stance on crypto and fintech regulation comes amidst increasing global focus on stablecoins. The Bank of England, for instance, launched consultations last year on regulating systemic stablecoins used in retail payments and wholesale settlement. Industry participants are closely watching the UK’s approach as a potential model for other jurisdictions.
Looking ahead, the UK plans to implement its broader crypto regulatory framework by 2027. With tokenization and stablecoins increasingly intersecting with traditional finance, these developments could significantly impact both fintech companies and institutional players aiming to expand in the digital asset space.

item.title: Crypto Hacks Reach $17B, Private Key Breaches Lead Losses | Over $17B was stolen in crypto hacks over 10 years, with private key compromises and phis... https://Blockchain.News/news/crypto-hacks-17b-private-key-breaches
item.description:
Original title: Crypto Hacks Reach $17B, Private Key Breaches Lead Losses Summary: Over $17B was stolen in crypto hacks over 10 years, with private key compromises and phishing attacks emerging as major vulnerabilities. Article text: Over $17B was stolen in crypto hacks over 10 years, with private key compromises and phishing attacks emerging as major vulnerabilities.
Crypto hackers have stolen over $17 billion across 518 incidents in the past decade, with private key compromises emerging as the leading attack vector, according to a report from analytics platform DefiLlama. The findings highlight a worrying shift in the focus of attackers from protocol-level vulnerabilities to weaknesses in wallet security and user behavior.
DefiLlama’s data reveals that 22.3% of these incidents stemmed from brute force attacks targeting private keys, while another 18.2% were attributed to unknown methods of key compromise. Phishing attacks on multi-signature wallets accounted for 10% of the cases. These techniques have eclipsed smart contract exploits in recent years, as developers have improved protocol-level security.
The latest example of this trend came just days ago, when Kelp DAO’s LayerZero-powered rsETH bridge was exploited for roughly $290 million in restaked Ether. This attack stands as the largest crypto theft of 2026 to date.
The decentralized finance (DeFi) sector has been particularly hard-hit by this new wave of exploits. A report from crypto trading firm GSR shows that over $600 million has been drained from DeFi protocols in just the past 60 days. The Kelp DAO hack and an April 1 exploit targeting the Solana-based Drift Protocol accounted for the lion's share of these losses.
GSR’s report also pointed to a troubling trend: attackers are increasingly targeting operational security, developer tooling, and signing infrastructure, areas that are often overlooked. With DeFi yields now compressing toward traditional finance rates, the risk-reward balance for depositing crypto on-chain is becoming a contentious question for users.
Advances in malware and artificial intelligence are making it easier for hackers to scale social engineering and wallet-targeting attacks. Dyma Budorin, CEO of cybersecurity firm Hacken, warned that “hacking-as-a-service” platforms on the dark web are enabling even low-skilled attackers to drain victims' wallets. These platforms often take a commission for providing tools, making wallet theft more accessible and lucrative.
According to Hacken, phishing and social engineering accounted for $306 million of the $482 million lost to Web3 hacks in Q1 2026. This suggests that even as user awareness grows, attackers are finding new ways to exploit human error and outdated security practices.
While phishing-related losses reportedly dropped sharply in 2025, thanks to increased user awareness, the rise of wallet-drainer scripts and sophisticated malware continues to present a significant challenge. For the industry, the focus now shifts to improving wallet and signing infrastructure security. Developers and users alike will need to adopt stronger operational security measures to fend off these increasingly “lazy” but effective attacks.
As the stakes rise, the crypto industry must confront a hard truth: better smart contract audits alone won’t solve the problem. The human and infrastructure elements of Web3 security are now the frontlines in a battle that shows no signs of slowing down.

As the stakes rise, the crypto industry must confront a hard truth: better smart contract audits alone won’t solve the problem. The human and infrastructure elements of Web3 security are now the frontlines in a battle that shows no signs of slowing down.
Over $17B was stolen in crypto hacks over 10 years, with private key compromises and phishing attacks emerging as major vulnerabilities.
Crypto hackers have stolen over $17 billion across 518 incidents in the past decade, with private key compromises emerging as the leading attack vector, according to a report from analytics platform DefiLlama. The findings highlight a worrying shift in the focus of attackers from protocol-level vulnerabilities to weaknesses in wallet security and user behavior.
DefiLlama’s data reveals that 22.3% of these incidents stemmed from brute force attacks targeting private keys, while another 18.2% were attributed to unknown methods of key compromise. Phishing attacks on multi-signature wallets accounted for 10% of the cases. These techniques have eclipsed smart contract exploits in recent years, as developers have improved protocol-level security.
The latest example of this trend came just days ago, when Kelp DAO’s LayerZero-powered rsETH bridge was exploited for roughly $290 million in restaked Ether. This attack stands as the largest crypto theft of 2026 to date.
The decentralized finance (DeFi) sector has been particularly hard-hit by this new wave of exploits. A report from crypto trading firm GSR shows that over $600 million has been drained from DeFi protocols in just the past 60 days. The Kelp DAO hack and an April 1 exploit targeting the Solana-based Drift Protocol accounted for the lion's share of these losses.
GSR’s report also pointed to a troubling trend: attackers are increasingly targeting operational security, developer tooling, and signing infrastructure, areas that are often overlooked. With DeFi yields now compressing toward traditional finance rates, the risk-reward balance for depositing crypto on-chain is becoming a contentious question for users.
Advances in malware and artificial intelligence are making it easier for hackers to scale social engineering and wallet-targeting attacks. Dyma Budorin, CEO of cybersecurity firm Hacken, warned that “hacking-as-a-service” platforms on the dark web are enabling even low-skilled attackers to drain victims' wallets. These platforms often take a commission for providing tools, making wallet theft more accessible and lucrative.
According to Hacken, phishing and social engineering accounted for $306 million of the $482 million lost to Web3 hacks in Q1 2026. This suggests that even as user awareness grows, attackers are finding new ways to exploit human error and outdated security practices.
While phishing-related losses reportedly dropped sharply in 2025, thanks to increased user awareness, the rise of wallet-drainer scripts and sophisticated malware continues to present a significant challenge. For the industry, the focus now shifts to improving wallet and signing infrastructure security. Developers and users alike will need to adopt stronger operational security measures to fend off these increasingly “lazy” but effective attacks.
As the stakes rise, the crypto industry must confront a hard truth: better smart contract audits alone won’t solve the problem. The human and infrastructure elements of Web3 security are now the frontlines in a battle that shows no signs of slowing down.
